content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The students sat down quietly when the teacher walked into the ___.
distractor_rationale: campus refers to the whole school grounds, not a single room where a teacher walks in to start a lesson
college is a type of institution, not a room that a teacher walks into for a lesson
degree is a qualification awarded after study, not a physical space

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Maria opened her book and started reading the first ___ of the story.
distractor_rationale: essay is a piece of writing a student produces, not a division of a book
exam is a test, not a section of a book
certificate is an official document awarded for completing something, not a part of a book

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom studied hard all week and felt ready for the maths ___ on Friday morning.
distractor_rationale: essay is a written piece of work, not a formal test taken on a specific day
chapter is a section of a book, not a test
course is a programme of study lasting weeks or months, not a single event on Friday

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: After passing all her exams, Sara received a ___ showing she had completed the cooking programme.
distractor_rationale: degree is awarded after a multi-year university programme, not a short cooking programme
assignment is a task given to a student during a course, not a document received after completing one
campus is a physical location, not a document awarded for completing a programme

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The teacher gave the class a written ___ to complete at home and hand in by Thursday.
distractor_rationale: exam is taken in a controlled setting at school, not given to complete at home and hand in
certificate is an official document awarded after completing a course, not a task set by a teacher
degree is a university qualification, not a piece of homework

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: James signed up for an online ___ in photography that lasts eight weeks and includes video lessons.
distractor_rationale: exam is a single test, not an eight-week programme with video lessons
essay is a written piece of work, not a structured programme of study
classroom is a physical room, not something you sign up for online

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The university ___ has a large library, a sports centre, and several cafes for students.
distractor_rationale: classroom is a single room, not a large area containing a library, sports centre, and cafes
course is a programme of study, not a physical location with buildings and facilities
chapter is a section of a book, not a place with buildings and facilities

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: After three years of lectures, exams, and research projects, David finally earned his ___ in biology from the university.
distractor_rationale: certificate is awarded for shorter programmes, not a three-year university research-based programme
assignment is a single task set during a course, not the qualification awarded at the end of it
course is the programme of study itself, not the qualification you earn by completing it

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Anna wrote a five-hundred-word ___ arguing that schools should give students less homework each night.
distractor_rationale: exam is a test you sit and answer questions in, not a piece of writing you compose with an argument
chapter is a section of an existing book, not an original piece of writing produced by a student
assignment is the general task set by a teacher; essay is the specific written form described by five hundred words and an argument, making assignment too vague and incorrect here

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The report showed that children whose parents had received a good ___ were more likely to do well in school themselves.
distractor_rationale: course refers to a specific programme of study, not the broad lifelong process of learning described in the report
campus is a physical location, not something parents receive that influences their children's success
certificate is a specific document for completing one programme, not the broad concept of learning and schooling referred to in the report

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The teacher wrote the new words on the board at the front of the ___.
distractor_rationale: a campus is the whole area of a school or college, not a single room with a board at the front
a degree is a qualification awarded after study, not a physical room where a teacher writes on a board
a course is a programme of study, not a room with a board where a teacher writes

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Maria studied hard all week and passed her maths ___ with a very high score.
distractor_rationale: an essay is a piece of writing you submit, not something you sit and receive a score for on a single occasion
a chapter is a section of a book, not something you pass with a score
a certificate is the document you receive after completing a course, not the test itself that you sit and score

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The students had to hand in their history ___ by Friday, so they spent three evenings writing and researching.
distractor_rationale: an exam is sat in a room under timed conditions, not handed in after several evenings of writing and researching at home
a certificate is an official document awarded on completion of a course, not a piece of work that students write and hand in
a campus is the physical area of a school or college, not a piece of written work that can be handed in

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: After finishing her three-year programme at university, Anna received her ___ in business at a formal ceremony.
distractor_rationale: an assignment is a piece of work set by a teacher, not a qualification received at a formal ceremony after three years
a course is the programme of study itself, not the qualification awarded at the end of it at a ceremony
a class is a single lesson or group of students, not a qualification presented at a formal ceremony

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The professor asked each ___ to submit a written application and attend an interview before being accepted onto the doctoral programme.
distractor_rationale: a student is already enrolled and studying, whereas someone who must apply and be accepted through an interview process is a candidate who has not yet been admitted
a college is an institution, not a person who submits an application and attends an interview
an assignment is a piece of work, not a person who can submit an application or attend an interview

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The students sat at their desks in the ___ and listened to the teacher.
distractor_rationale: campus refers to the whole school grounds, not the room where students sit at desks and listen to a teacher
a library is a place to read and borrow books, not where students sit at desks and listen to a teacher
an office is a workplace for staff, not a room with student desks and a teacher

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom opened his book to ___ five and began reading about animals.
distractor_rationale: a lesson is a period of teaching, not a numbered section inside a book
a course is a series of lessons or a programme of study, not a section inside a book
an essay is a piece of writing produced by a student, not a numbered section inside a book

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Maria studied hard all week because she had a maths ___ on Friday morning.
distractor_rationale: a degree is a qualification awarded after completing a university programme, not a test taken on a specific morning
a certificate is a document showing a qualification, not a test taken on a specific morning
an assignment is written work given by a teacher to complete at home, not a formal test taken in class on a set day

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: After finishing the cooking ___, Anna received a document showing she had passed all the required tests.
distractor_rationale: a degree is awarded specifically by a university after several years of study, not after a short cooking programme
an assignment is a piece of work set by a teacher, not a document received after passing tests
a class is a session of teaching, not a document received after completing a programme

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The teacher asked the students to hand in their ___ on the causes of pollution by Thursday.
distractor_rationale: an exam is a formal test taken in a room under timed conditions, not a piece of writing handed in by a deadline
a chapter is a section of a book, not a piece of student writing that is handed in
a campus is the physical grounds of a school or university, not a piece of student writing

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: David signed up for an online ___ in web design that lasts twelve weeks and includes weekly video lectures.
distractor_rationale: a degree requires years of full university study and cannot be described as a twelve-week online programme
a classroom is a physical room for teaching, not a programme of study that someone signs up for
an essay is a written assignment, not a programme of study that includes video lectures

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The professor told the ___ to submit their research reports before the end of the semester.
distractor_rationale: teachers are the ones who give instructions, not the ones told to submit reports to a professor
campuses are physical locations and cannot be told to submit reports
chapters are sections of a book and cannot be told to submit reports

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Sara moved into student accommodation on ___ during her first week at the university so she could walk to lectures easily.
distractor_rationale: college refers to an institution, not a physical location where accommodation is situated and from which lectures can be walked to
class refers to a session of teaching, not a location where accommodation is situated
course refers to a programme of study, not a physical location where student accommodation is found

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: After three years of study and a final dissertation, James was awarded a ___ in business management by the university.
distractor_rationale: a certificate is awarded after shorter programmes and is not specifically granted by a university following three years of study and a dissertation
an assignment is a task set during a course, not a qualification awarded at the end of three years
a chapter is a section of a book, not a qualification awarded by a university

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The government launched a programme to ___ children in rural areas where there were no schools or trained teachers.
distractor_rationale: examine means to test someone's knowledge formally, which does not address the absence of schools or teachers
assign means to give someone a task or role, not to provide schooling to children who lack access to it
graduate means to complete a course of study successfully, not to provide teaching to children who have no schools

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: My sister wants to be a ___. She loves working with children. (TEACH)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The ___ started at nine o'clock, so the students sat down quickly. (CLASS)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The school gave each student a ___ to show they had completed the course successfully. (CERTIFY)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The professor said that all ___ work, including essays and projects, must be submitted by Friday. (ASSIGN)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The results showed that students who attended every lecture performed better on ___ tasks than those who studied alone. (ACADEMY)
